events.label: assistant
events.text:
Assessment complete — this is a clear sequence violation. Written to `/app/answer.json`:

- **Decision: fail** (high risk) — the entry permit expired while the confined space entry was still active, but work continued anyway instead of stopping.
- Per OSHA 1910.146(e)(6), permit expiration requires immediate cancellation of the permit and evacuation; entry cannot legally continue on an expired permit.
- Correct sequence: stop work → evacuate all entrants → cancel the expired permit → reassess hazards → re-test the atmosphere (top/middle/bottom) → re-brief attendant/supervisor → issue a signed new permit → only then resume entry.
- Only non-entry, exterior support tasks (standby log, rescue equipment checks, ventilation staging, monitor calibration) are workable until the new permit is issued.
